name: Apple Pie-Topsy Turvy
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 9-inch pie pan, combine brown sugar, margarine, and corn syrup; spread evenly over the bottom of the pan.
2. Arrange pecans over the mixture in the pan.
3. Prepare the pie crust according to package directions for a two-crust pie; place the bottom crust over the mixture in the pan.
4. Preheat oven to 425°F.
5. In a small bowl, combine sugar, 2 tablespoons flour, and cinnamon; mix well.
6. Arrange half of the apple slices in the pie crust-lined pan; sprinkle with half of the sugar mixture.
7. Repeat with the remaining apple slices and sugar mixture.
8. Top with the second crust; seal and flute the edges.
9. Cut slits in several places around the top crust.
10. Bake at 425°F for 8 minutes or until apples are tender and the crust is golden brown. (Place the pan on foil or a cookie sheet during baking to catch any spillage.)
11. Loosen the edge of the pie; carefully invert onto a serving plate. Serve warm or cold with whipped cream.
